# Unit Tests for `truncate` Function

```python
import pytest
from your_module import truncate  # Replace with actual module name


class TestTruncateHappyPath:
    """Tests for normal, expected usage."""
    
    def test_string_shorter_than_max_len_returns_unchanged(self):
        """String shorter than max_len should be returned as-is."""
        assert truncate("hello", 10) == "hello"
    
    def test_string_equal_to_max_len_returns_unchanged(self):
        """String equal to max_len should be returned as-is."""
        assert truncate("hello", 5) == "hello"
    
    def test_string_longer_than_max_len_gets_truncated(self):
        """String longer than max_len should be truncated with default suffix."""
        assert truncate("hello world", 8) == "hello..."
    
    def test_truncate_with_custom_suffix(self):
        """Should use custom suffix when provided."""
        assert truncate("hello world", 8, ">>") == "hello >>"
    
    def test_truncate_with_empty_suffix(self):
        """Should work with empty string as suffix."""
        assert truncate("hello world", 5, "") == "hello"
    
    def test_truncate_with_long_custom_suffix(self):
        """Should handle custom suffix longer than default."""
        assert truncate("hello world", 10, " [...]") == "hell [...]"


class TestTruncateEdgeCases:
    """Tests for boundary conditions and edge cases."""
    
    def test_empty_string(self):
        """Empty string should return empty string."""
        assert truncate("", 5) == ""
    
    def test_max_len_zero(self):
        """max_len of 0 should return only suffix."""
        result = truncate("hello", 0)
        assert result == "..."
    
    def test_max_len_equals_suffix_length(self):
        """When max_len equals suffix length, no content is included."""
        result = truncate("hello world", 3, "...")
        assert result == "..."
    
    def test_max_len_less_than_suffix_length(self):
        """When max_len is less than suffix length, result is shorter than max_len."""
        result = truncate("hello world", 2, "...")
        assert result == "..."
        assert len(result) > 2  # Result exceeds max_len
    
    def test_single_character_string(self):
        """Single character string shorter than max_len."""
        assert truncate("a", 5) == "a"
    
    def test_single_character_string_needs_truncation(self):
        """Single character string that needs truncation."""
        assert truncate("a", 1) == "..."
    
    def test_unicode_characters(self):
        """Should handle unicode characters correctly."""
        assert truncate("café", 10) == "café"
        assert truncate("café au lait", 6) == "ca..."
    
    def test_whitespace_only_string(self):
        """String with only whitespace."""
        assert truncate("     ", 3) == "..."
    
    def test_newlines_and_special_chars(self):
        """Should handle strings with newlines and special characters."""
        text = "hello\nworld\t!"
        assert truncate(text, 20) == text
        assert truncate(text, 5) == "he..."


class TestTruncateErrorCases:
    """Tests for error conditions and invalid inputs."""
    
    def test_negative_max_len(self):
        """Negative max_len should still work (returns suffix)."""
        result = truncate("hello", -1)
        assert result == "..."
    
    def test_none_string_raises_type_error(self):
        """None as string should raise TypeError."""
        with pytest.raises(TypeError):
            truncate(None, 5)
    
    def test_none_max_len_raises_type_error
